_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
## LEED Arc Platform (O+M)

Facilities pursuing or maintaining LEED v4.1 O+M certification must log performance data continuously via the Arc platform across five categories: energy, water, waste, transportation, and human experience. Recertification is required every three years. For the workspace as a tenant in a LEED-certified facility, the workspace's operational data (energy consumption, waste generation, transport movements) will feed into the building's Arc performance score. Legal Confirmation Required: whether tenant data submission obligations are written into specific lease agreements.
*Source: USGBC, "Applying LEED to Warehouse and Distribution Center Projects," March 2026.*

## US Mandatory Benchmarking (NYC, LA)

Where the workspace occupies facilities in NYC buildings >25,000 sqft or LA buildings >20,000 sqft, the building owner is required to file annual energy benchmarking data via ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager. Legal Confirmation Required: whether tenant energy consumption data must be provided to building owners to enable this filing, and whether specific lease clauses impose this obligation.
*Sources: NYC LL84/97, https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/benchmarking.page; LA EWEO, https://www.ladbs.org/services/green-building/existing-buildings; ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager, https://www.energystar.gov/buildings/benchmark*

## UK MEES

No registration obligation currently falls on tenants. The EPC compliance obligation rests with the landlord. However, where the workspace is a tenant and the building fails to meet the required EPC rating, the lease may not be legally let or renewed. Legal Confirmation Required on current EPC rating of any UK logistics facilities occupied, before lease renewal.
*Source: UK MEES Guidance, https://www.gov.uk/guidance/domestic-private-rented-property-minimum-energy-efficiency-standard-landlord-guidance*
